2606.08123.title: Human-Centered Benchmarking of Driver Monitoring Models
2606.08123.paper_summary: Vision-based driver monitoring systems are increasingly deployed in safety-critical intelligent transportation settings, yet they are almost always compared on classification accuracy alone. This paper argues that accuracy is insufficient to characterize a model's fitness for real-world deployment, and proposes the Human-Centered Benchmarking Framework (HCBF), which evaluates models across four dimensions: accuracy, explainability, efficiency, and robustness. The framework is applied to four representative lightweight architectures, MobileNetV3, ShuffleNetV2, EfficientNet-B0, and DeiT-Tiny, on the MRL Eye Dataset for eye-state classification. While the models are nearly indistinguishable on clean-set accuracy, each leads in exactly one dimension, and all four lie on the Pareto frontier. A Human-Centered Score computed under three deployment-oriented weighting scenarios ranks ShuffleNetV2 first throughout. However, this aggregate winner retains less than half of its performance under sensor noise and fails by classifying closed eyes as open, whereas the transformer remains robust. These findings show that aggregate ranking can mask dimension-specific vulnerabilities that are operationally decisive, underscoring the value of multi-dimensional, human-centered evaluation.
2606.08123.paper_summary_zh: 基於視覺的駕駛者監控系統在安全關鍵的智能交通環境中越來越多地被部署，但它們幾乎總是僅在分類準確性上進行比較。本文主張，準確性不足以表徵模型在現實世界部署中的適用性，並提出了以人為中心的基準框架（HCBF），該框架從準確性、可解釋性、效率和穩健性四個維度評估模型。該框架應用於四種具有代表性的輕量級架構，MobileNetV3、ShuffleNetV2、EfficientNet-B0 和 DeiT-Tiny，針對 MRL Eye Dataset 進行眼睛狀態分類。雖然這些模型在乾淨數據集的準確性上幾乎無法區分，但每個模型在其中一個維度上領先，且所有四個模型都位於帕累托邊界上。在三種以部署為導向的權重場景下計算的人本中心分數中，ShuffleNetV2 始終排名第一。然而，這個綜合獲勝者在傳感器噪聲下的表現保持不到一半，並且在將閉眼分類為睜眼時失敗，而Transformer則保持穩健。這些發現表明，綜合排名可能掩蓋在操作上具有決定性意義的特定維度脆弱性，強調了多維度、人本中心評估的價值。
2606.08123.author: Ruben Dario Florez-Zela et.al.
